title: Webhook Management as a Service
story: As a product administrator, I want to create and manage webhooks for different event types so that external systems can receive real-time notifications when metrics are added, updated, or deleted.
description:
Implement a customer-facing webhook management system. After evaluating options, the team chose to build a native webhook UI rather than using Svix (which was initially recommended by architects for security reasons).

Customers can create webhooks for specific event types:
- Add Metric
- Update Metric
- Delete Metric

Webhooks are scoped to a team (account) and fire HTTP POST requests to customer-specified endpoints when the corresponding events occur. This feature is gated as a premium/upgraded license feature.

The longer-term vision includes a native Zapier integration so customers don't need to manage webhook endpoints directly.
acceptance_criteria: User can navigate to /webhooks to manage webhook subscriptions
User can create a new webhook with a target URL and select event types
Webhooks fire on metric create, update, and delete events
Webhooks are scoped to the user's team (account)
Feature is restricted to premium/upgraded license tier
Webhook list shows all active subscriptions with event types
ux_changes: New webhook management page at /webhooks where users can create, view, and manage webhook subscriptions. Includes event type selection and endpoint URL configuration.
middleware_changes: Event-driven webhook dispatcher that fires HTTP POST requests to registered endpoints when metric CRUD events occur. Webhook payloads include the metric data and event metadata.
data_model_changes: New webhooks table with relationship to teams (accounts), storing endpoint URL, event types, and active status.
api_changes: Outbound webhook POST calls to customer-defined endpoints. Internal CRUD endpoints for webhook subscription management.
implementation_notes: Initially considered using Svix (recommended for security and orchestration), but the team built a native solution. Feature is gated behind a premium license check. Only available for production-like environments currently.
comment_summary: Developer built the webhook UI with event triggers for metric CRUD. Product owner decided to gate this as a premium feature. The feature was enabled on development as a premium-only feature.
